title: A Hot Planet with a Surprising Blanket of Air
summary: Scientists used a big space telescope called the James Webb Space Telescope to look at a faraway planet named TOI-561 b. This planet is very hot and old, and it is called a super Earth because it is bigger than Earth. It orbits very close to its star, so one side is always bright and hot. People thought such a small, hot planet would lose its air quickly, but the telescope found signs that it has a thick atmosphere, like a blanket of gas around it. This is surprising because in our Solar System, hot small planets usually don't keep their air. The planet also has a lower density, which means it is less heavy than expected, and the atmosphere might help explain this. The telescope measured the temperature and found it cooler than if there was no air, showing heat is being moved around. This discovery helps us learn about planets in other parts of space.

keywords.explanation: a big telescope in space that helps scientists see faraway planets
a planet that is bigger than Earth but smaller than giant planets
the layer of gas around a planet, like air on Earth
the path a planet takes around a star
how heavy something is for its size

questions.question: What is the name of the telescope used to study the planet?
What is TOI-561 b called because it is bigger than Earth?
Why is it surprising that TOI-561 b has an atmosphere?
options: Because it is small and hot
Because it is made of gas
questions.correct_answer: Because it is small and hot

questions.question: What does the planet's lower density mean?
options: It is less heavy than expected
questions.correct_answer: It is less heavy than expected

questions.question: How did the telescope measure the planet's temperature?
options: By looking at its brightness in near infrared light
questions.correct_answer: By looking at its brightness in near infrared light

questions.question: What does the cooler temperature suggest about the planet?
options: Heat is being moved around by an atmosphere
questions.correct_answer: Heat is being moved around by an atmosphere

background_read: Space is full of planets outside our Solar System, called exoplanets. Some are like Earth, but others are very different. Scientists use telescopes to learn about them. The James Webb Space Telescope is a new tool that helps see details like atmospheres. An atmosphere is important because it can protect a planet and hold air for life. This article talks about a hot planet that keeps its air, which is exciting for space exploration.
Article_Structure: WHO: A team of astronomers led by Carnegie Science
WHAT: Found an atmosphere on a hot super Earth planet using the James Webb Space Telescope
WHERE: Around a planet called TOI-561 b, far away in space
WHY: To learn about planets in other solar systems and why some keep their air

perspectives.description: This shows how new technology like the James Webb Space Telescope helps scientists make surprising finds about planets.
